deck.title: Atelier
deck.tagline: Editorial pacing, paper surfaces, and restrained process language.
deck.introSubtitle: Editorial process deck inspired by deck.gallery

overview.title: What Is a Brand Ecosystem?
overview.subtitle: An airy editorial framing of process, voice, and visual cues
overview.summary: This sample mirrors the spacious editorial deck style: restrained palette, poetic headings, compact body copy, and one image-led focal block per slide.

cards.title: Quiet Surface
cards.body: Warm paper backgrounds and thin rules let typography do most of the work.

cards.title: Measured Accent
cards.body: Sage carries primary emphasis while ochre and terracotta act as small punctuation marks.

cards.title: Image Anchor
cards.body: Each spread needs one visual block that stabilizes the page and stops the layout from floating away.

cards.title: Process Language
cards.body: Headings sound like chapters, not dashboards: How This Works, Step 8, The Process.

talkingPoints: Keep dense copy inside one bounded column.
Use accent color in short bars, labels, and numerals rather than large fills.
Let the serif headline carry the personality.
overview.callout: The deck feels curated because color is quiet and hierarchy does the talking.

process.title: The Process
process.subtitle: A gentle, chapter-like flow from brief to approval
process.callout: This cycle should read like an editorial spread first and an operating model second.

works.title: How This Works
works.subtitle: A sample content slide with one large image block and three compact proof cards

cards.title: Step 1
cards.body: Set the premise with a short editorial intro rather than a technical explainer.

cards.title: Step 2
cards.body: Use an anchored photo or collage to carry texture and credibility.

cards.title: Step 3
cards.body: Resolve with a confident sentence in the accent color family.

works.callout: The image should feel placed, not decorated — it is the counterweight to the typography.

language.title: Language Bank
language.subtitle: Suggested phrases to keep the deck in the same editorial register

cards.title: Section Titles
cards.body: The Process, How This Works, Step 8, Presentation.

cards.title: Support Phrases
cards.body: Guided sequence, editorial pacing, restrained accent, image-led spread.

cards.title: Callout Style
cards.body: Short, quotable, and warm — more magazine than product sheet.

cards.title: Numeral System
cards.body: Use numbered chapters as visual anchors: 01, 02, 03…

language.callout: Use language with rhythm. The tone is composed, not hyped.
